maining 15 are Female. They are also sub-divided into
three groups as Divine, Human and Demoniac. The first group
of Divine asterisms consists of the nine stars, nos. 1,5, 7, S, 13,
15, 17, 22 and 27. The Human group is made up of 2, 4. 6, 11,
12, 20, 21, 25 and 26. The remaining stars belong to the
Demoniac class. They are also classified as Antarahga (of the
Inner Circle) and Bahiranga (of the Outer Circle). The First class
consists of 16 stars of four groups of four stars each: Krttika
and the next three, Magha and the three next, Anuradha and
the three next and Dhanistha and the succeeding three. The Outer
Circle consists of four triads headed by Punarvasu, C itra, U.
Asadha (including Abhijit in the middle), and Revati. The stars
arc further classified on the basis of their face, sight, and
characteristics. They are I) 1 acingupward, 2) Facing Downward

and 3) Facing Sideways. Their Sight is Manda (Dull). Madhya
